observedBehavior: A configured chart contains a symbol, timeframe, drawn price zones/channels, multiple indicators, watchlist context, and split-layout state.
The top-right Save control opens a layout menu with Save layout, Autosave, Share layout, Make a copy, Rename, Download chart data, Create new layout, and a recently used saved-layout list.
The layout picker can switch between one chart and multi-chart grids while preserving a configured chart environment.
The intended value is restoring a prepared chart workspace instead of manually rebuilding indicators, layout, symbols, and view settings each time.

currentProjectFindings.currentSaveSurface: The command bar already has a Save button and manage-layout caret menu.
currentProjectFindings.currentGap: Save opens a placeholder feature dialog. Manage-layout rows do not persist or restore layout state.
currentProjectFindings.existingPersistencePattern: Indicator templates already use localStorage under procharting.indicatorTemplates.

why: The QA app is a client-only Next.js harness with no existing backend database or server auth model.
Saved chart layout snapshots are small because candles are intentionally excluded.
localStorage matches the existing indicator-template persistence pattern and avoids adding unrelated dependencies.
The snapshot schema is plain JSON, so it can be migrated to IndexedDB or a server table later.

implementationPlan: Add SavedChartLayout and SavedChartPaneSnapshot interfaces near existing template types.
Load saved layouts from localStorage on mount with validation and fallback to an empty list.
Create a current-layout snapshot from existing app state, excluding candles/loading/feed/crosshair drag state.
Persist new snapshots through Save and Create new layout flows.
Restore snapshots by setting layout id, sync toggles, theme/style/settings, indicators, active pane, and pane states with fresh loading state.
Replace placeholder Save dialog with a name input and layout summary.
Render recently saved layouts in the manage-layout menu with open and delete controls.
Document the persistence model in ARCHITECTURE.md and verify in browser devtools.
qaPlan: Use Browser/Playwright to open the app, create a multi-pane modified layout, save it, mutate the chart, restore the saved layout, and inspect localStorage.
Check console errors and horizontal overflow after restore.
Confirm candles reload through existing market-data flow rather than being read from storage.
